channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [BAJAJELEC] Bajaj Electricals Ltd. Receives GST Act Assessment Orders for FYs 2019-20 and 2022-23 in Tamil Nadu
item.description:
Bajaj Electricals Ltd. has received two assessment orders under Section 74 and Section 73 of the Tamil Nadu GST Act, 2017, for FYs 2019-20 and 2022-23, respectively. The orders, received on March 13, 2026, demand a total of Rs. 19.93 crore (comprising tax demands, interest, and penalties) for both financial years. The company is currently evaluating legal options, which include filing an appeal before the appropriate appellate authority or a writ petition before the Hon’ble High Court against the orders. The financial impact of the orders, if they materialize, would be limited to the extent of the demand raised and penalty levied thereunder. There is no impact on the financial, operational, or other activities of the company on account of these orders.

  • Bajaj Electricals Ltd. received two assessment orders under the Tamil Nadu GST Act, 2017, for FYs 2019-20 and 2022-23.
  • The orders demand a total of Rs. 19.93 crore, comprising tax demands, interest, and penalties for both financial years.
  • The company is evaluating legal options, including filing an appeal or a writ petition against the orders.
  • The financial impact of the orders, if they materialize, would be limited to the extent of the demand raised and penalty levied thereunder.
  • There is no impact on the financial, operational, or other activities of the company on account of these orders.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BAJAJ ELECTRICALS LTD.-$ | Category: Legal

item.title: [SCOOBEEDAY] Scoobee Day Garments Re-Lodges Transfer Requests of Physical Shares
item.description:
Scoobee Day Garments (India) Ltd. has re-lodged transfer requests of physical shares for the period from February 05, 2026 to February 28, 2026. The re-lodgement is in accordance with the SEBI Circular No. HO/38/13/11(2)2026-MIRSD-POD/ 1/3750/2026 dated January 30, 2026. The report was received from the Registrar and Share Transfer Agent, M/s. Cameo Corporate Services Limited.

  • Scoobee Day Garments re-lodges transfer requests of physical shares
  • Re-lodgement is in accordance with SEBI Circular No. HO/38/13/11(2)2026-MIRSD-POD/ 1/3750/2026 dated January 30, 2026
  • Report received from Registrar and Share Transfer Agent, M/s. Cameo Corporate Services Limited
  • Period of re-lodgement: From February 05, 2026 to February 28, 2026

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Scoobee Day Garments (India) Ltd | Category: Monthly Update

item.title: [YOGI] YOGI Ltd: Secretarial Auditor M/s. Nishant Bajaj & Associates Resigns
item.description:
YOGI Ltd, formerly known as Parsharti Investment Ltd., has announced the resignation of its Secretarial Auditor, M/s. Nishant Bajaj & Associates, with effect from 14th March, 2026. The resignation is due to other pre-occupations and commitments. The auditor has stated that there are no material reasons for the resignation and has conveyed no objection to the appointment of a new Secretarial Auditor. The Audit Committee and the Board will consider the appointment of a new auditor in due course.

  • YOGI Ltd's Secretarial Auditor, M/s. Nishant Bajaj & Associates, has resigned.
  • The resignation is effective from 14th March, 2026.
  • The resignation is due to other pre-occupations and commitments.
  • The auditor has stated that there are no material reasons for the resignation.
  • The Audit Committee and the Board will consider the appointment of a new auditor in due course.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: YOGI Ltd | Category: Management Change

item.title: [ASTERDM] Aster DM Healthcare Faces Temporary Disruption in Kerala Hospitals Due to Nurse Strike
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Aster DM Healthcare Ltd. has experienced a temporary disruption in its operations across hospitals in Kerala due to a statewide nurses' strike led by the United Nurses Association. The strike, which began on March 9, 2026, has resulted in reduced occupancy levels and skeletal nursing staffing in most of the company's units. The company has taken internal contingency measures and mobilized nurses from other units to ensure continuity of critical services and patient care. The Kerala Private Hospital Association approached the Hon'ble High Court of Kerala at Ernakulam, which directed the Nurses Association to defer the strike until March 19, 2026, and refer the hospitals and nurses to the mediation centre for negotiation. This order has provided relief to the company, enabling uninterrupted operations during this period. Aster DM Healthcare anticipates a revenue loss of INR 23,01,63,509 due to the nurse strike, assuming the majority of nurses resume operations by March 19, 2026.

  • Aster DM Healthcare facing temporary disruption in operations due to a statewide nurses' strike in Kerala.
  • The strike, led by the United Nurses Association, has resulted in reduced occupancy levels and skeletal nursing staffing in most of the company's units.
  • Internal contingency measures and nurse mobilization from other units have been implemented to ensure continuity of critical services and patient care.
  • The Kerala High Court has directed the Nurses Association to defer the strike until March 19, 2026, and refer the hospitals and nurses to the mediation centre for negotiation.
  • Aster DM Healthcare anticipates a revenue loss of INR 23,01,63,509 due to the nurse strike.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Aster DM Healthcare Ltd | Category: Business Update

item.title: [SUNCITYSY] Suncity Synthetics Ltd to Consider Preferential Issue of Shares for Fund Requirement
item.description:
Suncity Synthetics Ltd has announced a Board Meeting on 20th March 2026 to consider and approve a Preferential Issue of shares. The company is looking to raise funds through various methods including equity shares, equity linked instruments, convertible loan, or any other securities. The fund raising will be in accordance with the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue of Capital and Disclosures Requirements) Regulations, 2018 and the Companies Act, 2013.

  • Suncity Synthetics Ltd to consider Preferential Issue of shares
  • Fund raising through various methods including equity shares, equity linked instruments, convertible loan, or any other securities
  • The fund raising will be in accordance with the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue of Capital and Disclosures Requirements) Regulations, 2018 and the Companies Act, 2013

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SUNCITY SYNTHETICS LTD. | Category: Investment

item.title: [SUNCITYSY] Suncity Synthetics Ltd Board Meeting to Consider Preferential Issue of Shares for Fund Requirement
item.description:
Suncity Synthetics Ltd has scheduled a Board Meeting on March 20, 2026, to consider and approve a Preferential Issue of shares to meet the company's fund requirements. The Board will also discuss other items as may be decided. The fundraising could be through issuance of equity shares, equity linked instruments, convertible loan, or any other securities as permitted by the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue of Capital and Disclosures Requirements) Regulations, 2018 and the Companies Act, 2013. This could be through private placement, qualified institutions placement, further public issue of debt or equity securities, preferential issue, right issue, or any other permissible mode.

  • Suncity Synthetics Ltd scheduled Board Meeting on March 20, 2026
  • Considering Preferential Issue of shares to meet fund requirements
  • Fundraising through issuance of equity shares or other securities
  • To be done through private placement, QIP, or further public issue
  • Decision to be made in accordance with SEBI and Companies Act, 2013 regulations

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SUNCITY SYNTHETICS LTD. | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [SHILINDORE] NCLT Disposes Insolvency Petition Against Sayaji Hotels (Indore) Ltd Following Settlement
item.description:
🔥 Trending

The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) has disposed of a petition filed under Section 9 of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 by M/s Ujaas Energy Limited against M/s Sayaji Hotels (Indore) Limited. The petition was filed due to non-payment of operational debt. However, following a settlement agreement between the parties, Ujaas Energy Limited applied for withdrawal of the case on February 27, 2026, which was accepted by the NCLT on March 11, 2026. The proceedings under Section 9 of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 have now been closed, marking the conclusion of the matter.

  • NCLT disposes insolvency petition against Sayaji Hotels (Indore) Ltd
  • Petition filed under Section 9 of Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 by Ujaas Energy Limited
  • Settlement agreement reached between Ujaas Energy Limited and Sayaji Hotels (Indore) Ltd
  • Ujaas Energy Limited applied for withdrawal of the case on February 27, 2026
  • NCLT accepted the application and ordered to dispose of the petition on March 11, 2026

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Sayaji Hotels (Indore) Ltd | Category: Legal

item.title: [BANCOINDIA] Banco Products Declares Second Interim Dividend of Rs. 8/- Per Share for FY 2025-26, Withholding Tax Communication Sent to Shareholders
item.description:
Banco Products (India) Ltd. has declared a second interim dividend of Rs. 8/- (i.e. 400%) per equity share having a face value of Rs. 2/- each for FY 2025-26. The dividend will be paid to the shareholders who hold equity shares of the Company as on the Record Date i.e. 19th March, 2026. The Company will deduct tax at source (TDS) on the dividend payable to shareholders as per the Income Tax Act, 1961. The TDS rates and conditions vary for resident and non-resident shareholders. The Company has sent an email communication to all the shareholders whose email IDs are registered with the Company/Depositories, explaining the process and documentation required for claiming exemption/withholding tax on the second interim dividend.

  • Banco Products declares a second interim dividend of Rs. 8/- per equity share for FY 2025-26.
  • The dividend will be paid to the shareholders who hold equity shares of the Company as on the Record Date i.e. 19th March, 2026.
  • The Company will deduct tax at source (TDS) on the dividend payable to shareholders as per the Income Tax Act, 1961.
  • The TDS rates and conditions vary for resident and non-resident shareholders.
  • The Company has sent an email communication to all the shareholders whose email IDs are registered with the Company/Depositories, explaining the process and documentation required for claiming exemption/withholding tax on the second interim dividend.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BANCO PRODUCTS (INDIA) LTD.-$ | Category: Dividend

item.title: [ASIANHOTNR] Vistra ITCL (India) Ltd Submits Disclosure for Asian Hotels (North) Ltd Under Takeover Regulations
item.description:
Vistra ITCL (India) Ltd, in its capacity as the debenture trustee, has submitted a disclosure under Regulation 29(1) of SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares & Takeovers) Regulations, 2011 for Asian Hotels (North) Ltd. The disclosure is in connection with the debenture trust deed and the non-disposal undertaking of Elana Holdings Pte. Ltd. The financings availed by the Target Company and Elana Holdings Pte. Ltd. are collectively referred to as the ‘Relevant Financings’. The undertakings being provided by Elana Holdings Pte. Ltd. over the shares and / or the securities of the Target Company held by it and the pledge created over the shares of Elana Holdings Pte. Ltd. issued by it to its shareholders are treated as an encumbrance under Takeover Code. The Target Company has also agreed that it shall procure that there will not be any change in its shareholding pattern without the prior written consent of the Debenture Trustee.

  • Vistra ITCL (India) Ltd has submitted a disclosure under Regulation 29(1) of SEBI Takeover Regulations for Asian Hotels (North) Ltd.
  • The disclosure is in connection with the debenture trust deed and the non-disposal undertaking of Elana Holdings Pte. Ltd.
  • The financings availed by the Target Company and Elana Holdings Pte. Ltd. are collectively referred to as the ‘Relevant Financings’.
  • The undertakings by Elana Holdings Pte. Ltd. are treated as an encumbrance under Takeover Code.
  • The Target Company has agreed not to change its shareholding pattern without the prior written consent of the Debenture Trustee.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Asian Hotels (North) Limited | Category: Deals

item.title: [SILVERTUC] Promoter Vipul Haridas Thakkar Acquires Shares in Silver Touch Technologies Ltd
item.description:
Vipul Haridas Thakkar, Promoter and Managing Director of Silver Touch Technologies Ltd, has acquired 4,000 equity shares on 11th March, 2026 and 400 equity shares on 12th March, 2026. The total holding of Mr. Thakkar before these acquisitions was 2,58,45,630 shares, which increased to 2,58,46,030 shares after the acquisitions.

  • Vipul Haridas Thakkar, Promoter and Managing Director of Silver Touch Technologies Ltd, has acquired shares in the company.
  • The total shares held by Mr. Thakkar before the acquisitions were 2,58,45,630, which increased to 2,58,46,030 after the acquisitions.
  • The acquisitions were made on 11th March, 2026 (4,000 shares) and 12th March, 2026 (400 shares).
  • The shares were acquired through the open market.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Silver Touch Technologies Ltd | Category: Investment
